plan:
- High-performance System-on-Chip (SoC) with integrated NPU (e.g., Rockchip RK3588S or similar ARM Cortex-A series) for AI and mobile computing.
- Custom PCB incorporating SoC, LPDDR5 RAM, NVMe storage, Wi-Fi 6E, Bluetooth 5.3, NFC, Sub-GHz transceiver (CC1101/CC1125), and multi-frequency RFID module.
- 4K AMOLED touchscreen display (approx. 5.5-6 inches) with MIPI DSI interface and advanced display driver IC.
- High-density Li-Po battery (3000-5000 mAh), USB-C Power Delivery controller, and efficient power management IC.
- Custom 3D-printed or CNC-machined enclosure (aluminum or high-strength polymer) with internal mounting points for components and antenna integration.
- Tactile buttons, scroll wheel, and compact multi-axis joystick for user input, alongside accelerometer/gyroscope sensors.

nodes.name: Main System-on-Chip
nodes.description: High-performance SoC for main operations, AI inference, display, and media processing.

nodes.name: LPDDR5 RAM Module
nodes.description: High-speed LPDDR5 RAM for system memory, connected directly to SoC.

nodes.name: Wi-Fi 6E & Bluetooth 5.3 Module
nodes.description: M.2 E-key module providing high-performance Wi-Fi 6E (802.11ax) connectivity.

nodes.name: Sub-GHz Transceiver (433/868/915 MHz)
nodes.description: Transceiver for Sub-1 GHz wireless communication.

nodes.name: Touchscreen Controller IC
nodes.description: Controller for the capacitive touch panel, enabling interactive navigation and text selection.

nodes.name: USB-C Power Delivery Controller
nodes.description: USB-C Power Delivery controller for charging and external power supply.

nodes.name: Power Management IC (PMIC)
nodes.description: Advanced power management IC for efficient charging and discharge of the LiPo battery.

nodes.name: Accelerometer & Gyroscope
nodes.description: 6-axis accelerometer and gyroscope for measuring orientation and angular velocity, crucial for balancing.

nodes.name: Power Button
nodes.description: Tactile button for 'Up' input.

nodes.name: Function Button 1
nodes.description: Tactile button for 'Up' input.

nodes.name: Function Button 2
nodes.description: Tactile button for 'Up' input.

nodes.name: Scroll Wheel Rotary Encoder
nodes.description: Rotary encoder for scroll wheel movement, without detents for smooth scrolling.

nodes.name: Compact Multi-Axis Joystick
nodes.description: Provides flight control input (e.g., thrust vectoring or rotation) for main MCU.

nodes.name: Front Enclosure Housing
nodes.description: Custom 3D-printed front shell, designed to hold the 4K AMOLED display and provide cutouts for user input components.

nodes.name: Rear Enclosure Housing
nodes.description: Custom 3D-printed rear shell, designed to house the main PCB, battery, and provide ventilation/antenna pass-throughs.

nodes.name: Display Bezel
nodes.description: Thin custom 3D-printed bezel to secure and frame the 4K AMOLED display within the front housing.

nodes.name: Main PCB Mount
nodes.description: Custom 3D-printed internal bracket to securely mount the main PCB within the rear enclosure.

nodes.name: Battery Holder
nodes.description: Custom 3D-printed holder to cradle and secure the Li-Po battery inside the enclosure, preventing movement.

nodes.name: Power Button Mount
nodes.description: Small 3D-printed part to hold and align the tactile power button within the enclosure opening.

nodes.name: Function Button 1 Mount
nodes.description: Small 3D-printed part to hold and align the tactile function button 1 within the enclosure opening.

nodes.name: Function Button 2 Mount
nodes.description: Small 3D-printed part to hold and align the tactile function button 2 within the enclosure opening.

nodes.name: Scroll Wheel Mount
nodes.description: Custom 3D-printed bracket to securely mount the scroll wheel encoder to the front housing.

nodes.name: Joystick Mount
nodes.description: Custom 3D-printed support for the compact multi-axis joystick, ensuring precise movement and secure attachment.

nodes.name: M2x6mm Mounting Screws
nodes.description: Small machine screws for securing internal components like PCBs and modules to 3D-printed mounts.

nodes.name: M3x10mm Enclosure Screws
nodes.description: Screws for fastening the front and rear enclosure halves together.

nodes.name: M2 Heat Set Inserts
nodes.description: Brass threaded inserts for creating strong thread points in 3D-printed parts for M2 screws.

nodes.name: M3 Heat Set Inserts

alternatives.name: McMaster-Carr M3 Brass Heat-Set Insert, 3.8mm Length
alternatives.reason: A shorter M3 brass heat-set insert option from McMaster-Carr, part number 94180A331 (M3-.5 Internal Thread, 3.8mm Length).

nodes.description: Brass threaded inserts for creating strong thread points in 3D-printed parts for M3 screws.

nodes.name: LPDDR5 RAM Module Mount
nodes.description: Adapter plate for the LPDDR5 RAM Module that bolts to the main chassis with M3 hardware. Includes wire-routing channels.

nodes.name: NVMe Internal Storage 256GB Mount
nodes.description: Adapter plate for the NVMe Internal Storage 256GB that bolts to the main chassis with M3 hardware. Includes wire-routing channels.

nodes.name: Wi-Fi 6E & Bluetooth 5.3 Module Mount
nodes.description: Adapter plate for the Wi-Fi 6E & Bluetooth 5.3 Module that bolts to the main chassis with M3 hardware. Includes wire-routing channels.

nodes.name: NFC Module Mount
nodes.description: Adapter plate for the NFC Module that bolts to the main chassis with M3 hardware. Includes wire-routing channels.

nodes.name: Sub-GHz Transceiver (433/868/915 MHz) Mount
nodes.description: Adapter plate for the Sub-GHz Transceiver (433/868/915 MHz) that bolts to the main chassis with M3 hardware. Includes wire-routing channels.

nodes.name: Multi-Frequency RFID Module Mount
nodes.description: Adapter plate for the Multi-Frequency RFID Module that bolts to the main chassis with M3 hardware. Includes wire-routing channels.

nodes.name: Touchscreen Controller IC Mount
nodes.description: Adapter plate for the Touchscreen Controller IC that bolts to the main chassis with M3 hardware. Includes wire-routing channels.

nodes.name: Accelerometer & Gyroscope Mount
nodes.description: Form-fitting cradle for the Accelerometer & Gyroscope that holds the sensing surface in its rated orientation. Snap-fit retention so it can be removed for calibration without tools.

originalPrompt: Create a custom and advanced product which will be same as and have the same capabilities as flipper pro, along with other most popular latest and greatest features and AI capabilities available in the current market. it should be not bigger in size than a 6 x 3 x 1 inch, have touchscreen advanced 4k display like an advanced smartphone, and will also be able to act as a mobile-computer.

subSteps.title: 3D print all enclosure parts and module mounts
Install M3 heat-set inserts into the rear enclosure housing
Install M2 heat-set inserts into front/rear enclosure housing and all printed mounts
Test fit the display into the display bezel and front enclosure
Solder the Main System-on-Chip (SoC) and Power Management IC (PMIC) to the main PCB
Solder the USB-C Power Delivery Controller and LPDDR5 RAM Module to the main PCB
Connect the NVMe Storage, Wi-Fi/Bluetooth, NFC, Sub-GHz, and RFID modules to the main PCB
Connect the Accelerometer/Gyroscope and Touchscreen Controller to the main PCB
Wire the display to the main PCB (MIPI_DSI) and connect the touchscreen I2C to the controller
Wire all tactile buttons, scroll wheel, and joystick to the main PCB
Connect the Li-Po battery to the PMIC and USB-C PD controller to PMIC
Perform initial continuity checks on all power and data lines
Connect external power via USB-C and verify power rails from PMIC to all modules
Flash initial bootloader and basic firmware to the Main SoC
Verify display output and touchscreen input functionality
Test basic functionality of NVMe storage, Wi-Fi/Bluetooth, NFC, Sub-GHz, RFID modules
Verify Accelerometer/Gyroscope and all button/input responses
Mount the Main PCB onto the main PCB mount using M2x6mm screws
Mount all electrical modules into their respective 3D printed mounts and secure to main PCB mount with M2x6mm screws
Mount the Li-Po battery into its holder and secure the holder to the rear enclosure with M2x6mm screws
Mount all buttons, scroll wheel, and joystick into their mounts and secure to the front enclosure with M2x6mm screws
Secure the display into the display bezel and then secure the bezel to the front enclosure with M2x6mm screws
Route all internal wires and carefully close the rear enclosure onto the front enclosure
Fasten the front and rear enclosure halves together using M3x10mm screws
Perform a final system function test to confirm all components are operational

projectDescription: An advanced pocket computer integrates Flipper Pro capabilities (sub-GHz, RFID, NFC) with a powerful SoC and AI, all displayed on a 4K AMOLED touchscreen. This 6x3x1 inch device functions as a portable mobile-computing platform, powered by a high-density Li-Po battery.

tools: 3D printer (PETG and ABS capable, with fine nozzle for 0.1mm layer height)
Tweezers (for small components and heat-set inserts)
assumptions: Builder has basic soldering skills for surface-mount components and pin headers
Access to a computer for flashing firmware and testing software
Familiarity with basic electronics and mechanical assembly principles
A suitable USB-C power adapter for initial power-up and charging
